clean_body: We need you to join us for mass calling and texting outreach to check in on how our neighbors are holding up, provide up to date public health information, and share contact information for grassroots groups in our area who are providing grocery delivery, | | | Hi Friend, As a lifelong community organizer, I knew that when the COVID 19 crisis hit, we had to devote whatever organizing resources we had to a grassroots response, focused on serving the needs and wellbeing of our community. is our initiative to activate our supporters to call and text their neighbors, regardless of political affiliation, all across southwest Oregon to support one another during this challenging time. We need you to join us for mass calling and texting outreach to check in on how our neighbors are holding up, provide up to date public health information, and share contact information for grassroots groups in our area who are providing grocery delivery, distributing hand sanitizer, expanded emergency food pantries, and more. I'm sure you have questions about how this works. We're adapting and building this as we go, and our first training is Please join us to learn: How to talk to your neighbors about the COVID 19 Crisis. How to use our power dialer, from home, to call 50 neighbors or more in one hour. How to use our text messaging platform to send 100 text messages+ an hour within your zip code. The key public health information we will be blasting through these networks. What grassroots groups and service agencies in our area are offering resources, and how to connect neighbors in need with those services. How you can help our community be resilient and connected in the face of this unprecedented crisis. This training will repeat weekly, or more frequently, as needed. We are building a network and we will provide the training to make this an easy and empowering way to help, right now. We are devoting the resources we have to connecting our community because we know that the only way through this crisis is together, and lives are at stake. This is a moment that will define us - as individuals, as leaders, and as a country. It's time to serve and to lead with our values. Now is the time for us to unite across our differences, check in on our friends and neighbors to share resources, and to make sure no one is left alone or left behind. Onward, Doyle Canning | Doyle Canning is running for Congress in Oregon's 4th Congressional District. Doyle is rejecting all corporate PAC money in this campaign. She relies on grassroots supporters like you! "Donate to Doyle Canning" | | Sent Via . name: =?utf-8?Q?The=20White=20House?=
subject: Americans need help. Democrats must stop blocking it.
body:
President Trump is marshalling every resource at America’s disposal to fight against the global Coronavirus pandemic. | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=d76f07e47c&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| The White House • March 23, 2020
---
| | |
# **Americans need help. Democrats must stop blocking it.**

**President Trump** is marshalling [every resource at America’s disposal](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=b0305d91eb&e=3ee4985eea) to fight against the global Coronavirus pandemic. He’s grateful to our nation’s local leaders, doctors, nurses, law enforcement, and first responders who are waging this battle on the ground.

**They need** all the help they can get—and so do American workers and businesses. Every American family deserves immediate relief and certainty in this difficult time.

**President Trump** told Congress to send him a bill that protects our economy, your job, your retirement savings, and your family's future from this global pandemic.

**Democrats are blocking it.** Their donor wish list comes before helping you.

**Senate Republicans** are ready to send a relief bill to the President’s desk. Instead of joining them, Democrats voted it down. Their leaders are demanding a litany of partisan line items that have nothing to do with fighting the Coronavirus or saving our economy.

**Speaker Nancy Pelosi** and Minority Leader Chuck Schumer should have to answer to every American: Why have they prioritized _these_ partisan issues over help for working families of every background?
* Audits of election results (yes, seriously)
* Airline carbon emissions
* Corporate diversity requirements
* Collective bargaining for Federal workers
* Expansion of wind/solar credits

**And the list** goes on and on—for 1,119 pages in Speaker Pelosi’s bill, to be exact.

**With these demands** , Democrats in Congress aren't trying to rescue our country. They aren't trying to rescue our economy. They're trying to rescue their partisan agenda—and they’re willing to hold America hostage to do it.

**As one senior Democrat** reportedly put it: This relief bill is “a tremendous opportunity to restructure things to fit our vision.”

**Now is no time** for partisan politics or to exploit a serious crisis for political gain. Now is the time to finish good-faith negotiations that deliver economic and healthcare support to working Americans.

**President Trump** and other leaders have asked every American to do their part to help slow the spread of Coronavirus by staying home and social distancing. The American people have selflessly heeded that call. As our citizens step up and do their part, it’s time for career politicians in Washington to do the same. No more delays.

**The clock is ticking.** The hour for action is here. Our country deserves it.

[“Is That What We’ve Come To?” asks Sen. Susan Collins ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=75632d63c7&e=3ee4985eea)

**MUST-READ:**[“Here is the Far-Left Wish List Nancy Pelosi Just Blew Everything Up For”](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=dccdf692e5&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
|

name: Lisa Scheller Campaign
subject: LISA SCHELLER CALLS ON SUSAN WILD TO DENOUNCE PElOSI’S PARTISAN GAMES DURING A TIME OF NATIONAL CRISIS
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Scheller for Congress, Inc
body:
Allentown, Pa – Lisa Scheller has released the following statement on the news
of Nancy Pelosi’s decision to upend vital emergency legislation for her

LISA SCHELLER CALLS ON SUSAN WILD TO DENOUNCE PElOSI’S PARTISAN GAMES DURING A
TIME OF NATIONAL CRISIS

FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE

Contact:

Dennis Roddy

press@schellerforcongress.com

March 23, 2020

Allentown, Pa – Lisa Scheller has released the following statement on the news
of Nancy Pelosi’s decision to upend vital emergency legislation for her own
partisan gain:

“Our national house is on fire and Nancy Pelosi and the House Democrats are
rushing to the scene with gasoline and kindling. After her Democratic allies in
the senate blocked vital economic recovery stimulus, Pelosi introduced
competing legislation packed with socialist Green New Deal policies and hidden
political tactics that have nothing to do with economic recovery, and
everything to do with political power and grandstanding.

“Her outrageous 1,119 page bill includes:

- A bailout of longstanding Post Office debt

- Required same-day voter registration

- Provisions on union collective bargaining

- An offset on airline carbon emissions

- A $15 minimum wage

- Retirement plans for newspaper employees

- Required early voting

- Permanent paid leave

- New and expensive airline regulations

- Expansion of wind and solar tax credits

“If not for this ongoing national emergency, any one of these proposals might
or might not merit debate in Congress in separate legislation. However, in the
presence of a national emergency with citizens sick and dying, Nancy Pelosi is
blowing up the economy and people's lives in order to advance their absurd
liberal wish list.

“This nation is the midst of a looming economic depression unlike anything we
have ever experienced. Holding this vital stimulus legislation hostage is
cynical, irresponsible, and a vivid example of why the American public is
losing faith in its institutions. Susan Wild has, to date, remained silent on
this outrageous partisan conduct, and has yet to explain whether she will
continue to ride shotgun with Nancy Pelosi as she drives the American economy
over a cliff.

“I call on Susan Wild to immediately denounce this behavior and urge Speaker
Pelosi to immediately take up the Senate bill once it arrives at the House
chamber for a vote.”

###

###

Paid for by Scheller for Congress, Inc.

After her Democratic allies in the senate blocked vital economic recovery stimulus, Pelosi introduced competing legislation packed with socialist Green New Deal policies and hidden political tactics that have nothing to do with economic recovery, and everything to do with political power and grandstanding. “Her outrageous 1,119 page bill includes: - A bailout of longstanding Post Office debt - Required same-day voter registration - Provisions on union collective bargaining - An offset on airline carbon emissions - A $15 minimum wage - Retirement plans for newspaper employees - Required early voting - Permanent paid leave - New and expensive airline regulations - Expansion of wind and solar tax credits “If not for this ongoing national emergency, any one of these proposals might or might not merit debate in Congress in separate legislation. However, in the presence of a national emergency with citizens sick and dying, Nancy Pelosi is blowing up the economy and people's lives in order to advance their absurd liberal wish list. “This nation is the midst of a looming economic depression unlike anything we have ever experienced. Holding this vital stimulus legislation hostage is cynical, irresponsible, and a vivid example of why the American public is losing faith in its institutions. Susan Wild has, to date, remained silent on this outrageous partisan conduct, and has yet to explain whether she will continue to ride shotgun with Nancy Pelosi as she drives the American economy over a cliff. “I call on Susan Wild to immediately denounce this behavior and urge Speaker Pelosi to immediately take up the Senate bill once it arrives at the House chamber for a vote.” Paid for by Scheller for Congress, Inc.

name: Tina Smith
subject: checking in
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Tina Smith for Minnesota
body:
|
#
I’m in Washington, working remotely from my apartment, doing everything I can to help complete a legislative package to help American families and small businesses respond to this unprecedented health and economic emergency. I’m fighting to get more tests and health care supplies to Minnesota, and my staff and I have talked to hundreds of Minnesotans to get your best ideas for what we can do right now to help. The last few weeks have been incredibly difficult for so many Minnesotans, and Americans. Our hearts go out to those who have lost loved ones, and those that are suddenly worried sick about losing their financial security. And also, we are overwhelmed with gratitude for all the people who continue to work so that the rest of us have the health care, food, medicines, and basic supplies we all need. I’m thinking of doctors and nurses and front line workers in health care and public safety, and the folks keeping child care centers running so these folks can go to work. I’m thinking of the front line workers in grocery stores, pharmacies, factories and everywhere else we turn to get what we need. I’m thinking of the farmers and ranchers, truck drivers and others working in food processing facilities and dairies — working to make sure we have food to put on the table. And I’m thinking of all the public servants in state, local and federal government, who have been working 24 hours a day to respond to this crisis. I want you all to know that I have never been more motivated to work for you in every way I know how to respond to this crisis in ways that put working families and small business first. In times of great challenge like this, we show our strength. We truly are in this together. Paul Wellstone’s simple but profound statement, “We all do better when we all do better” has never been more true. We have lots of work to do. Be careful out there. Tina **[P.S. I know this is an unprecedented time for many families, but if you have any change to spare, I hope you will consider pitching in to help an organization working on the frontlines of this pandemic. Please, only pitch in if you can afford to do so.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/16433814/174559737/-63012667?refcode=em200323-personal&amounts=10,25,50,100,150,250,1000&firstname=&lastname=&email=&addr1=&city=&state=&zip=&phone=&employer=&occupation=&amount=10&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83OTU1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmMjE4NzE4OS03NDZkLWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2IxZTgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=UpztE9bWF_CYArzSV00btCComA89WkS9QsXE_7z9BqU=)**
---

name: Tom Steyer
subject: Right now, we can lay the foundation for a cleaner future
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY TOM STEYER 2020]()
body:
| | | |
---
Americans are hurting, friend. In these uncertain times, it’s important that our government support the people who need it most, and I commend the made efforts by Democrats in Congress working to send direct financial assistance to people and small businesses in need as soon as possible. **As Congress continues to negotiate relief packages to address the impact of the coronavirus on American workers and our economy, we must not forget the potential impact on our planet.** Any stimulus packages passed by Congress will affect our country for years to come, so we have an opportunity to prioritize economic recovery that is sustainable. While the government works to backstop the economy, we cannot allow the COVID-19 pandemic to accelerate the climate crisis or worsen environmental injustices in our communities. This pandemic poses an immense challenge for individuals, families, and businesses — but we have an opportunity right now to lay the foundation for a better future. That is why I support Senators Whitehouse, Heinrich, Smith, Blumenthal, Markey, Booker, Stabenow, and Merkley who are working to ensure that financial assistance for airlines and cruise ships will transition us to a clean energy economy. _**[Join me by signing this petition to demand a stimulus package with people and climate at the center.](http://clicks.tomsteyer.com/ls/click?upn=M5l2K6CF-2BTeSi2jZtEX6WDq0JgL4SZ9fws6CW-2B-2BU1P2xJ41ARb0ToxQpL-2FaTtnEJl79q7IiS6sJSq-2BgV2-2BngVmsfoaAuO5IoeNpXtRRYU9zYN0E50p7YfsF6XllH2bU3FmHB7msXtyUze5-2BK98Mf1YrzfmT0gyCGO8argATUaCRbYduw3pPjkMaFM8MCgckDoNBesbWJHh-2FOdg8sh2RiwlWwKQSPX6wHGCGLiaGgvX4-3D8707_XwU9lPq5-2Bze3gI64ohlV0cQwWAuBAU7ADIvj2CS7ymjI06VISPfJIPkjxFld-2BrrlsLfWBgSznyC9eEQeuE67ByW7-2F4rW5t238CYEo3QD0YdN6GdfMXTdojDe-2FjGSSkqN1Afq32rWfJp-2BIfnll712U0jbFVqvrpplKZdpeNwpa-2FfzHcSjCguCDD98-2FIeSI23EMxut-2FKCNkecKpjW272hy0DBB0A-2FEsUd3y1QoeAq6cTNwWcU-2BjlTyvmz-2Bv36eQi3j7hC7auEeBFPbCFVcacEbbndYIC327B3q0szNs5PErrIvXYCXHD27IDXAQ6PuoI-2BVgluzLnFWFtMZnoukmuy-2B7GIbxIm2AihHvfZDaqJ-2B-2BBHpx9A-2B3GYNpoMnKHBo8Pbu9kL6NTvVvbyMxWPVSUOeo8cvQhYU1EKndsAfjHX-2FEE663ZXKKIeyzAfLIx0t96sfJT5SJoAM20JBJYO3pIeGLNHKs4WNaWUGBpLFL5oPIwE-3D)**_ **Here's what we need:**
* A stronger public health response that alleviates economic strain on individuals.
* Economic recovery for any industry, but especially the energy and transportation industries, must invest in long-term growth and sustainability. Financial assistance should be used to help these industries transition to a clean energy future, not double down on the fossil fuel economy of the past.
* The stimulus must require that industries continue to reduce air, water, and carbon pollution as a matter of public health — we cannot let one crisis intensify another.

**We need to ensure any new stimulus package protects working families and the health of our climate instead of big corporate profits.** We also can’t afford to be shortsighted in our recovery efforts. We need to be fair to the American people, protect small businesses, and include smart climate proposals that rebuild our economy and our planet. I hope you’ll join me in calling on Congress to do just that.
---

clean_body: | | | | Americans are hurting, friend. In these uncertain times, it’s important that our government support the people who need it most, and I commend the made efforts by Democrats in Congress working to send direct financial assistance to people and small businesses in need as soon as possible. As Congress continues to negotiate relief packages to address the impact of the coronavirus on American workers and our economy, we must not forget the potential impact on our planet. Any stimulus packages passed by Congress will affect our country for years to come, so we have an opportunity to prioritize economic recovery that is sustainable. While the government works to backstop the economy, we cannot allow the COVID-19 pandemic to accelerate the climate crisis or worsen environmental injustices in our communities. This pandemic poses an immense challenge for individuals, families, and businesses — but we have an opportunity right now to lay the foundation for a better future. That is why I support Senators Whitehouse, Heinrich, Smith, Blumenthal, Markey, Booker, Stabenow, and Merkley who are working to ensure that financial assistance for airlines and cruise ships will transition us to a clean energy economy. Here's what we need: A stronger public health response that alleviates economic strain on individuals. Economic recovery for any industry, but especially the energy and transportation industries, must invest in long-term growth and sustainability. Financial assistance should be used to help these industries transition to a clean energy future, not double down on the fossil fuel economy of the past. The stimulus must require that industries continue to reduce air, water, and carbon pollution as a matter of public health — we cannot let one crisis intensify another. We need to ensure any new stimulus package protects working families and the health of our climate instead of big corporate profits. We also can’t afford to be shortsighted in our recovery efforts. We need to be fair to the American people, protect small businesses, and include smart climate proposals that rebuild our economy and our planet. I hope you’ll join me in calling on Congress to do just that. | Forward, Tom | | "Facebook " | "Twitter " | "Instagram " ---|---|--- Please visit for information and resources to help slow the spread of COVID-19 and prevent infection. Follow CDC guidelines, including social distancing to avoid large groups and unnecessary contact with others, and adhere to instruction from your local and state elected officials.** We want to make sure you're receiving relevant information about how you can help us do what's right. If you’d rather not receive any emails from us, you can unsubscribe by clicking — we’ll be sad to see you go! Tom Steyer 2020 · PO Box 626 · San Francisco, CA 94104 · USA | [PAID FOR BY TOM STEYER 2020]()

name: Stacey Abrams
subject: Stacey Abrams: checking in and helpful resources
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Fair Fight Action
body:
|
---
[](https://secure.fairfightaction.com/page/m/41efa42f/34a064cc/1b579a4c/702b76a/157181410/VEsH/?g=yzW-IFH8x8VbBlZb5iRRXGA "https://fairfight.com/")

| | My fellow Georgians, As Georgia navigates the COVID-19 pandemic, I hope you and your loved ones are healthy and taking recommended precautions. These are uncertain times, but there are resources available to help protect your health and the well-being of those around you. I wanted to share some of these helpful resources. Anyone in Georgia with questions about COVID-19 should call the Georgia Department of Health's COVID-19 hotline at 844-442-268. For those who are in need or are looking to financially support great organizations helping Georgians, below are organizations on the front lines of this pandemic. With economic uncertainty looming for families across our state, these groups are helping keep food on their tables and provide support for those hit hardest by COVID-19. **Food Assistance**
* Food banks across Georgia are providing meals to those who need them, with precautions to protect health and safety. Find a food bank in your area here: [https://georgiafoodbankassociation.org/find-your-food-bank/ ](https://secure.fairfightaction.com/page/m/41efa42f/34a064cc/1b579a4c/702b76b/157181410/VEsE/?g=yzW-IFH8x8VbBlZb5iRRXGA "https://georgiafoodbankassociation.org/find-your-food-bank/")
* Foodfinder US is a resource for families to quickly find food assistance programs. To find food assistance near you, click here: [https://foodfinder.us/ ](https://secure.fairfightaction.com/page/m/41efa42f/34a064cc/1b579a4c/702b768/157181410/VEsF/?g=yzW-IFH8x8VbBlZb5iRRXGA "https://foodfinder.us/")
* For student meal pick up by school-district: [https://www.gpb.org/blogs/education-matters/2020/03/16/student-meal-pick-information-by-school-district ](https://secure.fairfightaction.com/page/m/41efa42f/34a064cc/1b579a4c/702b769/157181410/VEsC/?g=yzW-IFH8x8VbBlZb5iRRXGA "https://www.gpb.org/blogs/education-matters/2020/03/16/student-meal-pick-information-by-school-district")

**Resources for Service Workers**
* The Giving Kitchen provides emergency assistance to food service workers through financial support and a network of community resources. Learn more [here](https://secure.fairfightaction.com/page/m/41efa42f/34a064cc/1b579a4c/702b776/157181410/VEsD/?g=yzW-IFH8x8VbBlZb5iRRXGA "https://thegivingkitchen.org/covid19").
* The National Domestic Workers Alliance has set up a Coronavirus Care Fund for nannies, caretakers, and house cleaners to slow the spread of the virus by providing emergency assistance that enables domestic workers to stay home and stay healthy. Learn more [here](https://secure.fairfightaction.com/page/m/41efa42f/34a064cc/1b579a4c/702b777/157181410/VEsA/?g=yzW-IFH8x8VbBlZb5iRRXGA "https://www.domesticworkers.org/").

**Educational Resources**
* Georgia Public Broadcasting and the Georgia Department of Education have partnered to provide free broadcast programming and accompanying digital resources that adhere to the state's standards to provide continued at-home learning. You can access these resources [here](https://secure.fairfightaction.com/page/m/41efa42f/34a064cc/1b579a4c/702b774/157181410/VEsB/?g=yzW-IFH8x8VbBlZb5iRRXGA "https://www.gpb.org/education/learn").
* The Atlanta Journal-Constitution is providing in-depth coverage to keep you informed of how COVID-19 is impacting Georgia. [https://www.ajc.com/news/coronavirus/ ](https://secure.fairfightaction.com/page/m/41efa42f/34a064cc/1b579a4c/702b775/157181410/VEsO/?g=yzW-IFH8x8VbBlZb5iRRXGA "http://https://www.ajc.com/news/coronavirus/")

**Small Business Resources**
* Georgia small businesses can apply for an emergency loan through the federal Small Business Administration. You can find the application here: [https://disasterloan.sba.gov/ela/Account/Login ](https://secure.fairfightaction.com/page/m/41efa42f/34a064cc/1b579a4c/702b772/157181410/VEsP/?g=yzW-IFH8x8VbBlZb5iRRXGA "https://disasterloan.sba.gov/ela/Account/Login")

**Resources for Healthcare Workers**
* Hospitals in Georgia and across the country are in critical need of medical supplies in their efforts to fight COVID-19. Hospitals in rural Georgia are especially in need of help. Phoebe Putney Health System, which operates four hospitals in the Albany, Georgia area has online templates so you can make face masks at home. You can access more information and a list of needed medical supplies [here](https://secure.fairfightaction.com/page/m/41efa42f/34a064cc/1b579a4c/702b773/157181410/VEsHBQ/?g=yzW-IFH8x8VbBlZb5iRRXGA "https://www.phoebehealth.com/patients-and-visitors/coronavirus/mask-production").

I wish good health for you and those you love in these times of uncertainty. One thing to be certain of: If we work together we can fight this -- and we will win. Thank you and stay safe, -Stacey |
---|---|---

|
---
[](https://secure.fairfightaction.com/page/m/41efa42f/34a064cc/1b579a4c/702b770/157181410/VEsHBA/?g=yzW-IFH8x8VbBlZb5iRRXGA "https://www.facebook.com/FairFightAction/") [](https://secure.fairfightaction.com/page/m/41efa42f/34a064cc/1b579a4c/702b771/157181410/VEsHBw/?g=yzW-IFH8x8VbBlZb5iRRXGA "https://twitter.com/fairfightaction/") [](https://secure.fairfightaction.com/page/m/41efa42f/34a064cc/1b579a4c/702b77e/157181410/VEsHBg/?g=yzW-IFH8x8VbBlZb5iRRXGA "https://www.instagram.com/fairfightaction/")

Will you be a part of this historic movement? ""** We're counting on you to stick with us for the long-haul. If you want to stay updated but receive fewer emails, "" If you'd like to unsubscribe completely, we understand. Please unsubscribe "", and we'll make sure to process your request. People for Ben PO Box 25371 Albuquerque, NM 87125 | | Paid for and authorized by People for Ben.

name: Stacey Abrams
subject: Stacey Abrams: checking in and helpful resources
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Fair Fight Action
body:
|
---
[](https://secure.fairfightaction.com/page/m/41efa42e/34a064cc/1b51a022/702b79f/1713503775/VEsH/?g=yzW-IFH8x8VbBlZb5iRRXGA "https://fairfight.com/")

| | _(A previous version of this email had an incorrect number for the Georgia Department of Health's COVID-19 hotline. The number has been corrected below.)_ My fellow Georgians, As Georgia navigates the COVID-19 pandemic, I hope you and your loved ones are healthy and taking recommended precautions. These are uncertain times, but there are resources available to help protect your health and the well-being of those around you. I wanted to share some of these helpful resources. Anyone in Georgia with questions about COVID-19 should call the Georgia Department of Health's COVID-19 hotline at 844-442-2681. For those who are in need or are looking to financially support great organizations helping Georgians, below are organizations on the front lines of this pandemic. With economic uncertainty looming for families across our state, these groups are helping keep food on their tables and provide support for those hit hardest by COVID-19. **Food Assistance**
* Food banks across Georgia are providing meals to those who need them, with precautions to protect health and safety. Find a food bank in your area here: [https://georgiafoodbankassociation.org/find-your-food-bank/ ](https://secure.fairfightaction.com/page/m/41efa42e/34a064cc/1b51a022/702b79c/1713503775/VEsE/?g=yzW-IFH8x8VbBlZb5iRRXGA "https://georgiafoodbankassociation.org/find-your-food-bank/")
* Foodfinder US is a resource for families to quickly find food assistance programs. To find food assistance near you, click here: [https://foodfinder.us/ ](https://secure.fairfightaction.com/page/m/41efa42e/34a064cc/1b51a022/702b79d/1713503775/VEsF/?g=yzW-IFH8x8VbBlZb5iRRXGA "https://foodfinder.us/")
* For student meal pick up by school-district: [https://www.gpb.org/blogs/education-matters/2020/03/16/student-meal-pick-information-by-school-district ](https://secure.fairfightaction.com/page/m/41efa42e/34a064cc/1b51a022/702b79a/1713503775/VEsC/?g=yzW-IFH8x8VbBlZb5iRRXGA "https://www.gpb.org/blogs/education-matters/2020/03/16/student-meal-pick-information-by-school-district")

**Resources for Service Workers**
* The Giving Kitchen provides emergency assistance to food service workers through financial support and a network of community resources. Learn more [here](https://secure.fairfightaction.com/page/m/41efa42e/34a064cc/1b51a022/702b79b/1713503775/VEsD/?g=yzW-IFH8x8VbBlZb5iRRXGA "https://thegivingkitchen.org/covid19").
* The National Domestic Workers Alliance has set up a Coronavirus Care Fund for nannies, caretakers, and house cleaners to slow the spread of the virus by providing emergency assistance that enables domestic workers to stay home and stay healthy. Learn more [here](https://secure.fairfightaction.com/page/m/41efa42e/34a064cc/1b51a022/702b798/1713503775/VEsA/?g=yzW-IFH8x8VbBlZb5iRRXGA "https://www.domesticworkers.org/").

**Educational Resources**
* Georgia Public Broadcasting and the Georgia Department of Education have partnered to provide free broadcast programming and accompanying digital resources that adhere to the state's standards to provide continued at-home learning. You can access these resources [here](https://secure.fairfightaction.com/page/m/41efa42e/34a064cc/1b51a022/702b799/1713503775/VEsB/?g=yzW-IFH8x8VbBlZb5iRRXGA "https://www.gpb.org/education/learn").
* The Atlanta Journal-Constitution is providing in-depth coverage to keep you informed of how COVID-19 is impacting Georgia. [https://www.ajc.com/news/coronavirus/ ](https://secure.fairfightaction.com/page/m/41efa42e/34a064cc/1b51a022/702b7a6/1713503775/VEsO/?g=yzW-IFH8x8VbBlZb5iRRXGA "http://https://www.ajc.com/news/coronavirus/")

**Small Business Resources**
* Georgia small businesses can apply for an emergency loan through the federal Small Business Administration. You can find the application here: [https://disasterloan.sba.gov/ela/Account/Login ](https://secure.fairfightaction.com/page/m/41efa42e/34a064cc/1b51a022/702b7a7/1713503775/VEsP/?g=yzW-IFH8x8VbBlZb5iRRXGA "https://disasterloan.sba.gov/ela/Account/Login")

**Resources for Healthcare Workers**
* Hospitals in Georgia and across the country are in critical need of medical supplies in their efforts to fight COVID-19. Hospitals in rural Georgia are especially in need of help. Phoebe Putney Health System, which operates four hospitals in the Albany, Georgia area has online templates so you can make face masks at home. You can access more information and a list of needed medical supplies [here](https://secure.fairfightaction.com/page/m/41efa42e/34a064cc/1b51a022/702b7a4/1713503775/VEsHBQ/?g=yzW-IFH8x8VbBlZb5iRRXGA "https://www.phoebehealth.com/patients-and-visitors/coronavirus/mask-production").

I wish good health for you and those you love in these times of uncertainty. One thing to be certain of: If we work together we can fight this -- and we will win. Thank you and stay safe, -Stacey |
---|---|---
